PURPOSE
=======

The purpose of this policy is to ensure that users and technology system entities have a level of access to **YOTTABYTE** information systems (such as information on systems and applications) which is authorized, appropriate to their roles, and consistent with their responsibilities.

SCOPE
=====

This policy applies to all systems, people and processes that constitute the **YOTTABYTE** information systems, including all employees and other third parties who have access to **YOTTABYTE**’s information systems.

POLICY STATEMENTS
=================

It is **YOTTABYTE**’s policy to control access to its information systems to effectively protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of its classified data as follows:

Requirements of Access Control:
-------------------------------

*   Both internal and external Users and technology system entities should receive access to only the **YOTTABYTE’s** resources that are specifically authorized to them.
*   The provisioning of rights is performed in accordance with the principle of least privilege and on a need-to-know basis. The user’s manager is accountable for approval of the User rights required to perform the duties of the role or contract.
*   Information systems and data should be safeguarded from both internal and external unauthorized access.
*   Information System Network Access must be restricted to the authorized users and systems, using the principle of least privilege. 
*   Any access to network services like telnet, ftp, tftp, mail, etc. should be controlled, restricted, logged and monitored.

User Access Management:
-----------------------

*   A formal User on-boarding, update and off-boarding process shall be followed to authorize access and to facilitate assignment, change and revocation of rights.
*   When the User changes role and responsibilities, including extended leaves of absence, the assignment of rights should be reviewed and amended to ensure the principle of least privilege is maintained throughout.
*   User access must be removed in a timely fashion upon termination of employment or contract.
*   Each User must be assigned a unique user ID to enable Users to be held responsible for their actions. Credentials once assigned to a User cannot be shared and must be safeguarded by the accountable individual.
*   Access requests shall be approved by an appropriate authority (individual’s manager) prior to implementation by the IT systems and application administrators. 
*   An initial strong password shall be created on account setup and communicated to the user via a secure channel. The user must be required to change the password on the first use of the account.
*   Passwords should be protected with strong cryptographic controls during transmission and storage.
*   Each user shall be allocated with access rights and permissions to information systems and data that are commensurate with the tasks they are expected to perform. In general, this should be Role Based Access Control (RBAC).
*   Account and relative access rights shall be removed or disabled when a user no longer needs access due to changing roles or leaving **YOTTABYTE**.  
*   Ad-hoc additional permissions shall not be granted to user accounts; if such permissions are required this should be approved by the User’s manager.
*   When an employee leaves the organisation, their access to systems and data shall be suspended at the close of business on the employee’s last working day. This shall be part of the Offboarding Checklist that is given by the HR department which is to be completed and signed off from the respective department(s).

Privileged Accounts
-------------------

*   Privileged access to an information system shall be provided to users only if they have a need for such access as part of their job responsibilities and **YOTTABYTE** business needs.  
*   Every privileged account shall have a one-to-one relationship with an individual. 
*   Privileged access rights shall be controlled via multi-factor authentication.
*   Privileged access shall be promptly revoked when no longer required, upon job role changes, or upon the termination of employment. Termination of access will follow documented procedures to prevent unauthorized access.
*   Periodic access reviews will be conducted to ensure that privileges remain appropriate and necessary. Reviews will be documented and include relevant stakeholders.
*   In the event of a security incident involving privileged accounts, an incident response plan will be activated promptly to contain, investigate, and mitigate the incident.

Generic/Shared Accounts
-----------------------

*   Shared or generic accounts shall not be permitted unless a valid or business, justification has been assessed and approved by the relevant stakeholders.  
*   While providing access to Generic/shared accounts, the following information shall be captured:
    *   A responsible focal point is assigned to manage the account. 
    *   Purpose of account use.
    *   Names and contact details of individuals using the account. 
    *   Duration of account use i.e., temporary, or permanent. 
    *   Account passwords are reset when any individual no longer has a valid need to use the account. 
    *   Business or need of the account and associated individuals must be revalidated annually. 

Remote User Account
-------------------

*   Remote access to YOTTABYTE’s information systems shall be strictly controlled and implemented. Remote access is subject to the following restrictions:  
    *   Users are securely authenticated (via a username/password pair or where applicable using an additional, second authentication method) prior to the establishment of a remote connection. 
    *   Confidential data shall be encrypted when the data is transferred through public networks (i.e., Internet). 
    *   Remote access accounts with privileged rights for administrative use shall be limited to authorised personnel and strictly controlled via:  
        *   Two-factor authentication. 
        *   Regular monitoring of event logs.  
        *   Periodic reviews, at least annually, of access rights to verify the ongoing appropriateness of access rights. 
    *   Users shall not attempt to circumvent remote access (VPN) security controls. Any deviations must be addressed from a risk perspective and approved by the remote access and end point system business owner. 

Application Accounts
--------------------

*   The ownership and business purpose of functional accounts shall be maintained.
*   Access to Production business data should be restricted to the Business users and shall be controlled based on access rights allocated to each user. 
*   **YOTTABYTE’s** Application Administrator(s)/HR team / Employees(s) shall have access to the application such as HRMS as per their role allocated.
*   Access to the production environment of the application shall be appropriately controlled through proper access control.
*   Access to production environment such as RDS Database shall be strictly controlled.
*   Users shall connect to the databases using a VPN.

Local Administrator Access Rights
---------------------------------

*   Local administrative access rights shall be controlled as they allow an individual to have control over the operating system, and files on a specific computer.
*   A valid business justification shall be documented.

Secure Logon Procedures
-----------------------

*   **YOTTABYTE’s** employees shall be assigned a single user identifier to be used when accessing **YOTTABYTE’s** information systems.
*   User account management controls must be established to lock user accounts after a defined number of failed authentication attempts:

Account Management Characteristics

Account Management Policy

Account lockout threshold 

Ten (10) consecutive invalid password attempts 

Account lockout duration  

Thirty (30) minutes  

Maximum user screen locks out inactivity  

The maximum user inactivity timeout, requiring password re-entry, fifteen (15) minutes 

Password Management
-------------------

*   Passwords are mandatory for all user accounts on all networked and standalone information systems including operating systems, applications, databases, end user computing hardware and mobile devices (notebooks, tablets etc.):  

**Password Management Characteristics**  

**Password Policy** 

Minimum length ensuring passwords are not easily derived. 

Eight - fourteen (8-14) characters 

Complexity ensuring passwords are not easily derived. 

Alpha (upper & lower) and numeric characters and one special character (e.g., %, #) 

Maximum password age limiting the utility of a breached password. 

One hundred and eighty (180) days 

Minimum password age to prevent users from reverting to their old password immediately after an enforced password change. 

Five (5) passwords 

ANNUAL REVIEW
=============

The ISMS-related documents shall be reviewed once every year, or whenever the ISMS is updated. Internal Audit for the Information Security Management System based on the ISO standard(s) covered in the scope shall be carried out at least once a year and a report shall be submitted to the ISF (Information Security Forum). All the processes and procedures shall be reviewed at least annually to ensure compliance with the current changes and practices.

RELATED REFERENCE(S)
====================

**Related Standard(s)**

**Detailed Reference(s)**

ISO/IEC 27001:2022

Clause A.5.15- A.5.18, A.8.2, A.8.3
